sample.title: Can Cuba’s Economy Survive Trump?
sample.description:
Donald Trump’s blockade of Cuba, condemned by international groups, has caused shortages of fuel, food and medicine. Under threat from the US, will Cuba’s authoritarian government bend to his will? And as Havana seeks to rebuild a shattered economy, will it turn away from socialism and toward capitalism?

Read more on Bloomberg.com:

Cuba’s Bold Free-Market Turn Fails to Convince US It’s Ready to Change
https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-07-02/cuba-economic-crisis-trump-unconvinced-by-free-market-reforms?srnd=undefined

Watch more Weekly Docs: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLqq4LnWs3olXOhUOv4O6r-9OdWTQN83se

Everything you need to know about the big topics shaping our world, direct from Bloomberg's global newsroom.

#Cuba #Trump #economy

sample.title: Why McDonald's is supersizing in China
sample.description:
China is a major source of McDonald’s growth and it plans to have 10,000 locations in mainland China by the end of 2028.

CNBC's Eunice Yoon explains how the chain is winning over Chinese consumers with both value and nostalgia.

Find the full report in related video.

sample.title: This New Medicare Pilot Program Offers GLP-1s For $50 A Month. Here’s What You Need To Know
sample.description:
Americans on Medicare can now purchase GLP-1s for just $50 a month thanks to a temporary pilot program called Bridge.  Patients who meet certain requirements, like having  BMI of over 35 or having obesity-related conditions, may not qualify. CNBC’s Annika Constantino has more.

sample.title: ICE Could Sign A $220M Contract With A Taser Maker That Trump Invested Millions In
sample.description:
In February 2026, President Trump invested up to $5 million in top U.S. taser-maker Axon Enterprise. Two weeks later, ICE announced that it was seeking a $220 million contract to more than quadruple its inventory. Experts say that Axon is the only company able to meet the requirement outlined by ICE. CNBC’s Luke Fountain breaks it down.

Reporter: Luke Fountain
Produced by: Macklin Fishman
Edited by: Andrea Miller
Animation: Jason Reginato, Emily Park
Shot by: Charlotte Morabito
Senior Managing Producer: Shawn Baldwin

sample.title: Inside The Rise And Fall Of Kohl's
sample.description:
Kohl's rose to its peak as a department store in the 2000s, with a focus on a strong in-store experience, coupons and rewards. Now, after years of stagnant sales and a rough patch on Wall Street, Kohl's is trying to get back to what made it a household name.

Reporter: Laya Neelakandan
Produced by: Tasia Jensen
Animation: Jason Reginato, Emily Park

sample.title: BIO CEO John Crowley recalls seeing his daughter's X-rays
sample.description:
BIO CEO John Crowley describes seeing the before and after chest X-rays of his daughter, Megan.

"I’d seen this in other children, but to see it in your own kids was a real gift," he tells CNBC's Becky Quick.

sample.title: Apple shares up despite reportedly cutting down iPhone 17 production
sample.description: Apple shares pop despite a report that iPhone 17 production was cut 15%. Here's what investors are focused on instead. CNBC's MacKenzie Sigalos has the details.

sample.title: High-heeled runners celebrate Madrid LGBT Pride race
sample.description:
Dozens of participants wearing high heels sprinted through central Madrid on July 2 as part of the city's Pride week celebrations, drawing hundreds of spectators to the Chueca neighborhood.
#Madrid #LGBT #Pride #priderace #highheels

sample.title: World Cup reunites Peace Corps teacher with Cape Verde student
sample.description:
A World Cup watch party in Oakland, California reunited a former Peace Corps teacher with her Cape Verde student after decades apart. Jill Tucker, 61, served with the Peace Corps from 1990 to 1992 teaching English in Cape Verde, where Ivan Silva, 49, was her student. The pair spoke to Reuters about their reunion.
 
#WorldCup #reunited #teacher #CapeVerde #student

sample.title: US Olympian indicted over alleged Reflecting Pool vandalism
sample.description:
Former U.S. Olympic canoeist David Hearn has been indicted on a charge of felony destruction of property for allegedly vandalizing the newly renovated Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool in Washington, court records showed on July 2.

Massive Russian attack on Ukraine’s capital kills at least 25 and injures many more | BBC News
sample.description:
Russian forces have carried out a mass drone and missile attack on Ukraine’s capital Kyiv, killing 27 people, in what the city's mayor described as " massive attack" on the city.

Ninety-one people were injured according to the head of Kyiv's military administration.  Mayor Vitaly Klitschko said an ambulance station was among the places hit.

Some neighbourhoods had been evacuated as the airstrikes hit the city hours after Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky had warned that Russia was preparing an attack.

Moscow said its forces had hit what it called military plants in retaliation for attacks on Russian civilian infrastructure.
The Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov told reporters that Russia would "continue to increase pressure on the Kyiv regime in order to achieve our set goals".

Ukraine accused Moscow of targeting civilian areas and said it would be wrong to equate the actions of the "aggressor and a country defending itself".

Reeta Chakrabarti presents BBC News at Ten reporting by Sarah Rainsford.

Subscribe to our channel here: https://bbc.in/bbcnews

For the latest news download the BBC News app or visit BBC.com/news

#BBCNews
